summary: iter114 — Wheel Boost Event tested. Backend: 7/9 new integration tests pass; existing 46/46 wheel_fortune unit tests still green. Frontend: all required data-testid attributes verified in source (BoostBanner, aura, admin form, stats panel). E2E UI navigation blocked by Cloudflare Turnstile on preview domain (cannot fully drive Bob/admin through browser without bypass).

minor.issue: Multiplier x2 effect could not be observed end-to-end because Bob's cycle is auto-flagged suspicious (>3 spins/60s burst detection), which silently throttles points to 0 and forces slot=1 — making it impossible to validate gain_multiplier or perdu_reduction statistically through public API. DB inspection confirms boost_active=TRUE and boost_id correctly persisted on each spin.
Works as expected — confirmed total_spins/dau/by_slot returned correctly during active boost window.

integration_issues.flow: Wheel page load via preview domain
integration_issues.issue: Cloudflare Turnstile error 110200 blocks full E2E render in headless browser (Cloudflare WAF preview). Component code & testids verified in source instead.

action_items: Consider adding a unit test (in test_wheel_fortune.py style) that calls _apply_boost_to_distribution with phase 1 distribution + perdu_reduction_percent=95 and asserts slot 1 weight drops to 0 — this would close the statistical gap left by suspicious_flag throttling on the live path.
Optional: in wheel_spin, when user is suspicious AND boost is active, log the event distinctly for observability so admins can see boost effectiveness vs anti-fraud throttle interactions.
No frontend code changes required — testids, BoostBanner, aura conditional, admin form & stats panel all present per spec.
critical_code_review_comments: wheel_fortune.py is 2528 lines — exceeds 700-line guideline. Consider splitting into routes/wheel/{spin.py,admin.py,boost.py,cycle.py}.

get_active_wheel_boost() reads 7 admin_settings rows on every /wheel/status and every /spin call — consider TTL cache (already partly noted in settings_service docstring) to avoid N round-trips per request under load.

_apply_boost_to_distribution rounds to int — at perdu_reduction=99 weight=5 it returns 0 (slot dropped). At edge cases (weight*reduction near 0.5) Python round() uses banker's rounding which may cause minor distribution surprises. Acceptable for current product but worth documenting.
test_credentials: SuperAdmin emileparfait2003@gmail.com / Gerard0103@ (login → 2FA OTP via DB email_otps purpose=login_2fa, then POST /api/auth/verify-2fa). Bob bob@japap.com / Test1234!. Turnstile bypass token JAPAP_E2E_BYPASS_2026 (body field turnstile_token).
seed_data_creation: None — used existing Bob & SuperAdmin accounts; cleared email_otps + suspicious_flag between runs; final cleanup ran (boost disabled).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend boost endpoints and DB persistence are verified. The two flaky tests (spin/multiplier and perdu_reduction) hit anti-fraud throttling because rapid spins trigger burst detection → suspicious_flag → forced slot=1. To get clean statistical samples, either (a) run with a unique device_fingerprint per spin and clear suspicious_flag between each spin in the same SQL transaction the spin reads, or (b) add a dedicated unit test that calls _apply_boost_to_distribution() directly. Boost activation, partial PUT, ISO validation, bounds, boost_id minting on enable, /wheel/status boost block, /admin/boost/stats and disable flow are all green. Frontend testids match the spec exactly — admin can self-verify the UI by enabling the boost via admin tab and visiting /games/wheel as Bob.
rca of the issue: Statistical perdu_reduction test fails because: (1) Bob's wheel_cycles row gets suspicious_flag=TRUE auto-set when 3+ spins occur within 60s on the same device_fingerprint (security_service burst detection in routes/wheel_fortune.py:746-757); (2) the suspicious branch forces base_points<=25 AND maps slot_idx=0→1 (Perdu) for the rest of the cycle, contaminating any rapid statistical sample. Even after the test resets suspicious_flag to FALSE before each spin, the next spin's burst guard re-flags. The boost reduction logic itself (_apply_boost_to_distribution at line 469) is correct: with phase-1 weight 5 for slot 1 and reduction 95%, slot 1 is removed entirely from the weighted pool. The fix is test-side, not product-side: use a unique device_fingerprint per spin to bypass the 60s-burst guard.
